diagrams.title: Mathematical Induction Process Flow
diagrams.description: Shows the three-step process of mathematical induction
elements: Base Case (n=1): Prove P(1) is true
Conclusion: P(n) true for all n∈ℕ
diagrams.purpose: Visualize the chain of reasoning in induction proofs

diagrams.title: Domino Analogy for Induction
diagrams.description: Dominoes representing sequential truth of propositions
elements: Domino n (P(n)): Falls due to Domino n-1
All dominos fallen represents P(n) true for all n
diagrams.purpose: Understand why induction requires both base case and inductive step

diagrams.title: Strong vs Weak Induction Comparison
diagrams.description: Comparison of assumption scope in weak vs strong induction
elements: Weak Induction: Arrow from P(k) → P(k+1)
Strong Induction: Arrows from P(1),P(2),...,P(k) → P(k+1)
Shows strong uses more assumptions but both reach same conclusion
diagrams.purpose: Differentiate between weak and strong induction assumptions

diagrams.title: Binary Tree Structural Induction
diagrams.description: Recursive structure of binary tree for structural induction proof
elements: Base Case: Single node (leaf) - prove property
Recursive Case: Root with left and right subtrees
Inductive Step: If P(left) AND P(right), then P(whole tree)
diagrams.purpose: Apply structural induction to tree data structures
